label: Moodles
title: Moodles
lead: Planned documentation for moodle display and player condition indicators.
bodyHtml:
Status moodle bridge

CUCoreLib appends MoodleRegistry and queues custom entries and feeds them into the real vanilla MoodleManager during its normal update pass.

That means your custom moodle should behave like any other moodle. It'll needs a valid icon name, a display name, a description, an intensity that matches one of the background slots the game expects, and an important choice that decides whether it belongs in the main row or the side row.

When to add a moodle

Use a moodle when the player needs visible feedback about a custom status effect. Keep it lightweight. The normal pattern is to call MoodleRegistry.AddMoodle(...) each update while the condition is active; CUCoreLib keeps it visible briefly and avoids duplicate spam for the same warning.

MoodleRegistry.AddMoodle(
        1,
        someLoadedImageAsset,
        "Lead Poisoning",
        "You're feeling a bit woozy and fatigued...",
        critical: false,
        chippedOnly: false,
        important: true // optional, defaults to true
    );

The intensity value picks the same vanilla background frame used by the base game.

IntensityVisualNotes
0Lowest standard tier.
1Low warning tier.
2Moderate warning tier.
3Strong warning tier.
4Used by minor positive/sleep moodles.
5Higher positive or special tier.
6Strong positive or special tier.
7Special positive standard tier. (Life Support)
8Special last-tier background used by rare moodles. (Last Stand)
critical: trueCritical is an overlay behavior, not a separate intensity number
